title: TotalEnergies Earnings Undermines By High Finance Costs
description: TotalEnergies Marketing Nigeria Plc, published its third quarter unaudited financials performance for the period ended September 30, 2023, reporting a 48.9% decline in standalone Earning Per Shares (E
html:
TotalEnergies Marketing Nigeria Plc, published its third quarter unaudited financials performance for the period ended September 30, 2023, reporting a 48.9% decline in standalone Earning Per Shares (EPS ) to N5.99 against N11.71 in 2022 the lowest since fourth quarter of 2020 which was N5.14.

The earnings where affected mainly by an uptick in net finance cost that was up by 23.6%.

As a result, the nine months EPS settled lower at N31.87 against N36.83 in 2022.

Other highlights of the result shows that revenue grew by 15.4%, driven primarily by an increase in its Network sales.

Meanwhile, revenue declined across the general trade and aviation business segments.

Notably, analyst attribute the improved performance in the network segment to the increased prices of PMS, AGO, and DPK in the period, while the weak macroeconomic environment pressured demand across the general trade and aviation segments.

Analyzing the products classification, the marketer recorded broad base expansion from petroleum products and lubricants and other sales.

On a quarter to quarter basis, revenue increased by 6.2% following gains from the general trade and aviation business segments, despite a 2.6% quarter to quarter decline in the network segment.

Remarkably, the gross margin expanded by 266bps to 14.2%, as the revenue growth for the period outpaced the increase in the cost of sales.

The cost pressures in the period were influenced by the elevated crude oil prices.

Meanwhile, EBITDA and EBIT margins declined to 4.4% and 3.0%, respectively, following a 44.5% increase in operating expenses.

Net finance cost surged by 23.6% to NGN1.35 billion against N1.09 billion, due to a 69.2% increase in finance cost.

The increased finance cost was primarily facilitated by the higher balance in interest on import loans and other loans.

Meanwhile, finance income grew by 226.5% in the period supported by higher interest on deposits, interest on other loans, and interest on deposits for unclaimed dividends.

profit before tax declined by 48.0% to NGN3.15 billion against N6.05 billion in 2022. Following a tax expense of N1.11 billion against N2.07 billion in 2022, profit after tax stood at N2.03 billion against N3.98 billion in 2022.

The result highlights the inadequacies in the downstream oil and gas sector and the weakness in the operating environment which have remained inhibiting factors to petroleum marketers.

Industry watchers anticipate the company to see out the year with resilient momentum, as they envisage higher demand from festive induced activities.

Nonetheless, its expected that cost pressures will remain unabated over the rest of the year.
